## @PRINTMAXXER -- TWITTER

### Tweet 1 -- Coffee brewing streak blue ocean [9.5/10]
4.61 million people are in coffee communities online.

every app that exists for them: Acaia scale companion. Fellow kettle timer. static recipe references.

apps that say "day 12: try a 1:16 ratio with a 30-second bloom" and track your daily brewing streak: zero.

same gap for grilling (4.44M), cooking (4.41M), baking (3.44M), meal prep (3.42M).

20+ million people across food hobbies. zero habit-tracking apps. all passive content browsers.

habit apps convert to subscriptions. recipe browsers don't.

### Tweet 2 -- Bad business or bad marketing [9.3/10]
saw a post from a shop owner in Australia asking "do i have a bad business or just a marketing problem?"

unique product. real customers walking in. repeat buyers. instagram engagement.

but their website gets 12 visits a day. no SEO. no Google Business profile optimized. product photography shot on a phone with bad lighting.

the product works. the distribution is broken.

90% of "bad businesses" are actually good products with zero distribution. you don't need a better idea. you need someone to fix your Google listing.

### Tweet 3 -- Email deliverability pre-send check [9.2/10]
most people obsess over email subject lines and A/B testing.

meanwhile their emails aren't reaching the inbox at all.

one team started running every outbound email through a deliverability check before sending. simulates Gmail, Outlook, Yahoo. gives a spam score. flags authentication issues.

in 3 months they caught:
- 3 domains with misconfigured SPF
- emails landing in spam they never knew about
- authentication gaps that tanked their sender reputation

test BEFORE you send. not after your reply rate drops to 2%.

### Tweet 4 -- AI attribution in commits [9.0/10]
@swyx running a poll: should Codex add attribution to itself in your commits, like Claude does?

the real question nobody's asking: does your AI tool want credit or does it want to disappear?

Claude adds "Co-Authored-By: Claude" to every commit. Codex doesn't. Cursor doesn't. Copilot doesn't.

in 2 years every codebase will have AI fingerprints everywhere. the companies that made attribution default will look transparent. the ones that hid it will look shady.

attribution is the new open source signal.

### Tweet 5 -- Day 44 activation gap [9.4/10]
day 44. revenue: $0.

but here's what changed today: 8 Stripe payment links are live. 7 digital products have buy buttons. 47 apps have upgrade paths wired.

the gap was never "build more." it was "connect the thing that exists to the place where money changes hands."

44 days building. 1 day wiring payments. that ratio is wrong and i know it.

if you're a builder stuck at $0: stop building. open Stripe. create one product. paste the link somewhere a human can click it.

the system doesn't need more features. it needs a cash register.
